content.rendered:
Discussion Topic

Please reflect on your own cultural and linguistic background, religion, academic learning ability, learning styles, etc and how this has shaped and contributed to who you are as a person and/or a teacher/administrator/counselor/etc. Put together a story about your cultural identity in the form of a collage. It is the intention of this assignment for you to explore within yourself, your background, your language and your cultural disposition as a person teacher/administrator/counselor/etc and be able to share it in a way you might want your students to introduce and define themselves. In this assignment, I will grade on creativity, quality, comprehensiveness and focus in getting one’s story across. Use pictures, words, phrases, illustrations and more to create your collage. Do include a short paragraph describing what we will see in your collage.

Discussion Thread

Tina Liu

Oct 23, 2020 11:24pm


Ni Hao (Hello in Mandarin)!

Please enjoy a trip through my life this weekend – a pictorial autobiography of cultural exchange and promoting (personal, professional, and life-long passion) spanning 3 regions of the world, over half a century.  Thanks to the digital space that is virtually limitless, pun intended, the actual size of my collage is a small mural.  For a clearer read on the several media I’ve been on over the years, see the PDF attached.  Let it load up fully before you zoom.  It’s a big file that is viewable online only too.  The image embedded below is just a preview.  A more updated version with a few more labels to photos in the first reply of this posting is attached there. I‘m almost tri-lingual, English, Mandarin Chinese, and French more or less, and have a bit of each illustrated throughout my collage on the places I’ve been and lived.  Here’s an album with more photos of my time in France and Europe.  There are many more albums of beautiful destinations and events in Europe on my profile.  Feel free to browse through  This video is the feature interview on me in my birth country of Taiwan, where you’ll see some sights there, and more images of my life elsewhere too, as well as hearing some Mandarin and a bit of French.  Je vous souhaite un bon weekend (I wish you a good weekend in French)!

Sang-Ho Lee

Oct 25, 2020 10:34am


Hello Tina,

This is such a comprehensive collage! This reminds me that everyone has a story, and that there is so much we can learn and discover from each other. I’m also starting to think that it might be fun and … inspirational, maybe, to create a timeline collage of my life as a personal project and keep expanding it as I live on. 

Nice to get to know you better!


Tina Liu
Oct 25, 2020 7:56pm

여보세요 and Guten Tag Sang-Ho!  I’m glad you enjoyed my collage and to see that we share the same feelings about this collage being a personal project.  Why I spent so much effort to do mine.  Although most of it is on FB, somehow putting it all down on a one-pager really shows the whole journey. 


It’s great to see that we have a few things in common, having experienced Asia, the US, and Europe and being kinda trilingual, both our weaker one being the European ones.  I’m curious about why you were in Indonesia. Re the rainbow, if you are ever interested in VR environments, I have an art project called Unity Sanctuary in AltspaceVR where habitable lanterns are lit in rainbow colors.  There’s a photo of it at the bottom right corner of my collage.  You can explore worlds there with just a laptop, it’s free and there’s a big LGBTQ community there too.  Let me know if you’d like to have a guided tour.  I’d be happy to show you around   If you are on FB, please feel free to connect with me there.  I’m looking forward to getting to know you more!
